meta.title: Unit 2 — Common & Proper Nouns (Test 3)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Focus on correcting capitalization errors in sentences.

questions.question: Which of these is a proper noun?
questions.explanation: "Halloween" is a proper noun — it's a specific holiday name and must be capitalized.

questions.question: "City" is a common noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! "city" refers to any city — it's a common noun and does not need a capital letter.

questions.question: What type of noun is "Taipei"? (common or proper)
questions.hint: It's the name of a specific city
questions.explanation: Taipei is a proper noun — it's the specific name of a city.

questions.question: Which word is a common noun?
questions.explanation: "street" is a common noun — it refers to any street, not a specific one.

questions.question: "december" is correctly written.
questions.explanation: December is a month name — a proper noun. It must be capitalized: December.

questions.question: My teacher's name is ms. chen. Fix the name: write it correctly.
questions.hint: Both the title and surname need capital letters
questions.explanation: "Ms. Chen" is a proper noun — both the title (Ms.) and surname (Chen) need capital letters.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: holiday/newspaper/hotel = general words (common). Halloween/Sunday = specific names (proper).

questions.question: In the sentence "We sing songs on christmas," the capitalization is correct.
questions.explanation: "Christmas" is a proper noun (holiday name) and must be capitalized: Christmas.

questions.question: Which sentence has correct capitalization?
questions.explanation: "Ms. Lee" is a proper noun. Only proper nouns get capitals — "teacher" stays lowercase.

questions.question: What type of noun is "October"? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: October is a proper noun — months are specific names and must be capitalized.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "We sing songs on Christmas." Christmas is a proper noun.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: friend/girl/city = general words (common). Main Street/Taipei 101 = specific names (proper).

questions.question: "Wednesday" is a proper noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! Wednesday is a day of the week — days are proper nouns and must be capitalized.

questions.question: Which sentence has the correct capitalization?
options: There are so many people on main street.
There are so many people on Main Street.
There Are so many people on Main Street.
There are so many people on Main street.
questions.explanation: "Main Street" is a proper noun — both words in a place name get capitals.

questions.question: What type of noun is "Dragon Boat Festival"? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: Dragon Boat Festival is a proper noun — it's a specific holiday name.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "Ms. Lee is my teacher." Ms. Lee is a proper noun.

questions.question: In the sentence "The bear's name is max," which word has an error?
questions.explanation: "max" is a name — a proper noun. It must be capitalized: Max.

questions.question: In the sentence "They play in the Playground," the capitalization is correct.
questions.explanation: "Playground" is a common noun — it should NOT be capitalized. Only proper nouns need capitals.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: school/beach = general words (common). John/Monday/FamilyMart = specific names (proper).

questions.question: My birthday is in march. Fix the month: write it correctly.
questions.explanation: March is a proper noun (month name) — it must start with a capital letter: March.
